pattern: A single linear pass can decide whether a sequence ever rises and whether it ever falls; it is monotonic exactly when at most one of those two things happens.
hints: You do not need to know the direction in advance; just watch for any step that goes up and any step that goes down.
Track two booleans across one sweep of adjacent pairs: did the value ever increase, and did it ever decrease.
The array is monotonic precisely when it never increased or never decreased; equal neighbours count toward neither.

edge_inputs.description: all equal is both directions at once

stress.description: random short-range arrays so monotonic and non-monotonic both occur
stress.note: narrow range makes flat and mixed sequences frequent
swaps inject inversions into otherwise sorted data
